bead.title: Wire consumer workload gate into automation
bead.description:
Wire the consumer-workload runner into Axon's automated gates after the contract, core runner, Nexiq adapter, and DDx/Cayce status slots exist.

Required behavior:
- Add a cheap PR-safe check only if it cannot require sibling repos or long browser runs. Prefer runner self-test and Nexiq dry-run/contract-smoke gating when the local source is available.
- Add a nightly or workflow_dispatch job that can run the real Nexiq workload against Axon SQLite and Postgres, preserving artifacts.
- DDx and Cayce must not be silently green in CI. Missing or contract-gap statuses must be reported according to the status matrix from docs/helix/03-test/consumer-workload-gate.md.
- The workflow must upload summary.json/log artifacts or otherwise make them available in GitHub Actions.
- Avoid hardcoding /home/erik paths in GitHub Actions; use checkout paths, environment variables, or an explicit documented local-only mode.

In-scope files:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml and/or .github/workflows/nightly.yml
- scripts/run-consumer-workloads.sh only for small CI option adjustments
- docs/helix/03-test/consumer-workload-gate.md if the CI mode semantics need clarification

Out of scope:
- Changing release publishing.
- Editing sibling projects.
- Making full Nexiq Playwright E2E a required per-PR gate if it is too expensive for normal CI.
bead.acceptance:
1. `rg -n "run-consumer-workloads|consumer workload" .github/workflows scripts docs/helix/03-test/consumer-workload-gate.md` exits 0.
2. `scripts/run-consumer-workloads.sh --self-test` passes.
3. `cargo fmt --check` passes.
